rule_text: For messages that represent her professionally, Esther prefers a draft-first workflow instead of sending immediately.
canonical_answer: I always want professional messages drafted first so I can review the wording before anything goes out.
counterfactual_default: Send the message directly once the content is ready.

evidence.content: organized, data‑driven approach to EHR documentation

rule_text: When an invitation overlaps with work coverage or an unresolved commitment, Esther prefers to respond tentatively rather than accepting or declining right away.
canonical_answer: When my schedule is still in flux, I usually mark invites as tentative until I know I can fully commit.
counterfactual_default: Accept or decline the invitation immediately.

evidence.content: manage client schedules

rule_text: For group plans like potlucks, volunteer events, or teammate meetups, Esther prefers creating a shared calendar event rather than a private reminder.
canonical_answer: If other people are involved, I want it on the calendar as a real shared event, not just a personal reminder.
counterfactual_default: Set a private reminder instead of creating a calendar event.

evidence.content: they host monthly potluck nights at the senior center
volunteering at the senior center’s cultural events

rule_text: For scheduled commitments tied to exams, appointments, or outings, Esther prefers reminders that fire before the event instead of a generic repeating schedule.

rule_text: When organizing work-related files, Esther prefers classifying them by date rather than by project or owner.

rule_text: For dining plans, Esther prefers restaurants with strong Mexican or fusion flavors suitable for sharing over generic American options.

rule_text: When planning a getaway, Esther prefers stops that combine outdoor adventure with cultural immersion instead of a purely generic sightseeing list.
canonical_answer: For trips, I like an itinerary that mixes hiking or outdoor adventure with something culturally meaningful.
counterfactual_default: Choose stops based mainly on generic popularity or convenience.

evidence.content: prefers carefully planned weekend getaways that blend adventure and cultural immersion

rule_text: If a trip stop falls through, Esther prefers replacement stops that preserve the original adventure-or-culture theme rather than swapping in any convenient alternative.
canonical_answer: When part of a trip breaks, I want the replacement to match the original vibe, not just be the easiest substitute.
counterfactual_default: Replace the disrupted stop with any available high-rated alternative.

rule_text: For regional group travel, Esther prefers shared ground transport over air travel when it helps the group stay together and save money.
canonical_answer: On shorter regional trips with other people, I prefer shared ground travel so we can keep costs down and travel together.
counterfactual_default: Check flights first or choose the fastest air option.

rule_text: When booking lodging for group trips, Esther prefers hotel offers that support roommate-style cost sharing rather than private single-occupancy options.

counterfactual_default: Choose a standard private room for comfort or convenience.

rule_text: When she does fly, Esther prefers a window seat for the landscape views.

rule_text: When ordering ingredients or kitchen supplies, Esther prefers products that reflect locally sourced Pacific Northwest or traditional Mexican cooking over generic substitutes.

rule_text: When something she bought is wrong but still useful to her plans, Esther prefers an exchange rather than a refund.
canonical_answer: If an item is the wrong version but I still need it, I’d rather exchange it than just get my money back.

rule_text: When she needs to trim recurring costs without giving a service up entirely, Esther prefers switching to the basic plan.
